stem: The circle \( x^{2}+y^{2}+([[D]])x+([[E]])y+F=0 \) has center \( (h,k) \). Find \( h \).
The circle \( x^{2}+y^{2}+([[D]])x+([[E]])y+([[F]])=0 \) is written in general form. Find its radius \( r \).
solution: Completing the square gives center \( \left(-\frac{D}{2},-\frac{E}{2}\right) \) and \( r^{2}=\left(\frac{D}{2}\right)^{2}+\left(\frac{E}{2}\right)^{2}-F \), so \( r=\frac{\sqrt{D^{2}+E^{2}-4F}}{2}=[[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This forgets to multiply \( F \) by \( 4 \) before subtracting under the root.
This forgets to divide by \( 2 \) at the end (reports \( r^2\)'s square root of the doubled expression).
This adds \( F \) instead of subtracting it.
This reports \( r^{2}\) (without taking the square root) instead of \( r \).
This is off by one from the correct radius.
This is off by one (other direction) from the correct radius.

stem: The circle \( x^{2}+y^{2}+([[D]])x+([[E]])y+F=0 \) has center \( (h,k) \). Find the distance from the center to the origin.
solution: The center is \( \left(-\frac{D}{2},-\frac{E}{2}\right) \), so its distance to the origin is \( \sqrt{\left(\frac{D}{2}\right)^{2}+\left(\frac{E}{2}\right)^{2}}=\frac{\sqrt{D^{2}+E^{2}}}{2}=[[answer]] \); note this does not depend on \( F \).
distractor_rationales: This forgets to divide by \( 2 \) after taking the square root.
This squares and averages instead of using the distance formula correctly.
This uses \( |h|+|k|\) (taxicab distance) instead of Euclidean distance.
This uses a minus sign instead of a plus sign under the root.
This is off by one from the correct distance.
This is off by one (other direction) from the correct distance.

stem: A circle has center \( ([[h0]],[[k0]]) \) and radius \( [[r]] \). When its equation is written in the general form \( x^{2}+y^{2}+Dx+Ey+F=0 \), what is \( F \)?
distractor_rationales: This adds \( r^2 \) instead of subtracting it.
This swaps the roles of \( h^2+k^2 \) and \( r^2\), flipping the sign of the whole expression.
This negates \( h^2+k^2\) as well as \( r^2\).
This drops the \( k^2 \) term with a sign error.
This is off by one from the correct value of \( F \).
This is off by one (other direction) from the correct value of \( F \).

stem: The equation \( [[c]]x^{2}+[[c]]y^{2}+([[D]])x+([[E]])y+F=0 \) describes a circle. Divide through by the leading coefficient and complete the square to find the \( x \)-coordinate \( h \) of the center.
distractor_rationales: This forgets to divide by the leading coefficient \( c \) before completing the square.
This drops the required minus sign.
This multiplies by \( c \) instead of dividing by it.
This forgets the factor of \( 2 \) in the denominator.
This is off by one from the correct value of \( h \).
This is off by one (other direction) from the correct value of \( h \).

stem: Find the area enclosed by the circle \( x^{2}+y^{2}+([[D]])x+([[E]])y+([[F]])=0 \).
solution: Completing the square gives \( r^{2}=\frac{D^{2}+E^{2}-4F}{4} \), so the area is \( \pi r^{2}=\frac{\pi(D^{2}+E^{2}-4F)}{4}=[[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This reports \( \pi r \) (the circumference formula's radius factor) instead of \( \pi r^{2}\).
This forgets to divide by \( 4 \) after computing \( D^2+E^2-4F \).
This forgets to multiply \( F \) by \( 4 \) before subtracting.
This uses \( 2\pi r \) (circumference) instead of \( \pi r^2\) (area).
This is off by one from the correct area.
This is off by one (other direction) from the correct area.

stem: A circle in general form \( x^{2}+y^{2}+([[D]])x+([[E]])y+F=0 \) passes through the point \( ([[x0]],[[y0]]) \). Find the radius of the circle.
solution: Substituting the point gives \( F=-\left([[x0]]^{2}+[[y0]]^{2}+([[D]])([[x0]])+([[E]])([[y0]])\right)=[[F]] \). Then completing the square, \( r=\frac{\sqrt{D^{2}+E^{2}-4F}}{2}=[[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This forgets to divide by \( 2 \) at the end.
This adds \( 4F \) instead of subtracting it.
This uses the distance from the point to the origin instead of solving for \( F \) and completing the square.
This reports \( r^{2}\) instead of \( r \).
This is off by one from the correct radius.
This is off by one (other direction) from the correct radius.
